cards.back: A technique used to transmit data in a computer network by dividing it into fixed-length blocks, adding redundancy, and using error-correcting codes to detect and correct errors
A type of error-correcting code used in block coding, where the parity bits are added in a cyclic manner to the data block, allowing for efficient error detection and correction
A technique used to detect errors in data transmission by calculating a checksum or message digest, which is compared to a predetermined value to verify the integrity of the data
A specific type of cyclic code used for error detection and correction, which adds redundant bits to the data block to allow for single-bit error correction and double-bit error detection
